Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search the LOS for the six loan files assigned to Marcus Webb using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan numbers: LN-2026-00616, LN-2026-00539, LN-2026-00547, LN-2026-00631, LN-2026-00197, LN-2026-00596) or mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to matching Marcus Webb's staff ID los_staff_a583f044387a) to retrieve loan status, borrower info, processor assignments, rate locks, closing dates, and current pipeline state. This should surface: Destiny Pham (LN-2026-00616) in processing with rate lock expired 3/31 and assigned processor Rachel Kim (inactive), Emily Hassan (LN-2026-00539) in conditional approval with 3/30 closing and processor Sofia Reyes, Lakshmi Grant (LN-2026-00547) in underwriting with 3/28 closing and assigned processor Rachel Kim (inactive), Taylor Morgan (LN-2026-00631) in processing with no closing date and processor Darnell Price, Brian Harris (LN-2026-00197) in underwriting with no closing date and rate lock expired 3/18 and processor Darnell Price, and James Walker (LN-2026-00596) as closed.
OE 2: Pull document checklists on all six loans using mortgage_los_get_documents (filtered by the respective loan IDs) and check for any open conditions using mortgage_los_get_conditions. This should reveal: LN-2026-00616 (Pham) has multiple docs still in "required" status (urla, paystub_2, bank_statement_2, tax_return_prior, title_commitment) while the hoi_binder shows as approved on the checklist; LN-2026-00631 (Taylor Morgan) has a standard document checklist with no gift-specific document types the gift documentation issue (donor bank statement, Taylor's deposit statement) is not tracked in the LOS checklist and would need to be discovered via email, Slack, and CRM; LN-2026-00197 (Brian Harris) shows the appraisal checklist item as received the ongoing appraisal delay with Richard Pemberton is not reflected in the LOS checklist status and would need to be discovered via Slack and CRM. The mortgage_los_get_conditions calls return no condition rows for these loans; the lender PTC conditions and underwriting requirements are tracked in email and Slack communications rather than in the LOS conditions table.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Destiny Pham" or "LN-2026-00616" or "closing failure" or "Allison Mercer" or similar) to find the full history on the Pham file: (1) the lender PTC conditions email from Homestead Wholesale to Sofia Reyes requiring updated HOI binder by 3/17 or face suspension, (2) Allison Mercer's angry email to Carlos Rivera (CC: Grace) threatening counsel and broker escalation over the missed closing and lock expiration, (3) Grace Yamamoto's reply to Allison taking over escalation as single point of contact, (4) the appraisal report from Donna Whitfield showing value supports at $291,000 but flagging foundation cracking and crawl space water damage, and (5) Grace's email scheduling a pipeline review meeting with Sofia, Elena, and Carlos to walk workload and the missed condition timeline.
OE 4: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history (query: "LN-2026-00616" or "Destiny Pham" or "suspended" or similar, channel: C002 #loan-processing or C003 #closings) to find the processing channel history on the Pham file: Sofia flagging the HOI binder condition on 3/9, Sofia admitting on 3/14 she hasn't touched it and saying the lender due date is that day (note: the PTC conditions email from the lender states 3/17 as the deadline Sofia may be referencing an internal or informal earlier target), Camille noting the file flipped to suspended on 3/19 and referencing the lock as expired (note: Slack messages reference a 3/23 lock expiration while the LOS shows rate_lock_expiration as 3/31 this is a cross-service data discrepancy the agent would encounter), Carlos noting Destiny emailed asking if they're closing that day, Sofia admitting on 3/22 she missed it entirely (file suspended, lock dead, closing passed), Carlos flagging Allison's threatening email, Grace directing Carlos to keep borrower comms tight and not promise a close date or lock fix, and Grace moving the file to Elena Marchetti as new processor.
OE 5: Search emails and Slack using search_emails (query: "Taylor Morgan" or "LN-2026-00631" or "gift" or "donor statement" or similar) and conversations_search_messages (query: "Taylor" or "gift" or "donor" or "LN-2026-00631") to find the doc situation on the Morgan file: (1) Taylor's email to Elena confirming the signed gift letter for the $20K parent gift is already in the file, (2) Amy Chen's email to Taylor explaining underwriting still needs the donor bank statement showing the $20K withdrawal and Taylor's statement showing the deposit, (3) Amy's softer follow-up email explaining parents can redact unrelated transactions while still providing the required pages, (4) Slack messages from Amy (keystone_4d01ebef3a15) noting Taylor pushed back parents won't send statement citing privacy, and Elena (keystone_c6b42763fb1f) noting the donor statement is still not in and marked pending in the checklist, and (5) CRM engagement notes logging the gift docs condition and borrower pushback.
OE 6: Search Slack and emails using conversations_search_messages (query: "Pemberton" or "LN-2026-00197" or "appraisal" or "rural" or similar, channel: C002 #loan-processing) and search_emails (query: "Pemberton" or "appraisal" or "rural" or similar) to find the appraisal delay history on the Harris file. Note: the Harris/LN-2026-00197 appraisal delay story is primarily in Slack and CRM "Brian Harris" and "LN-2026-00197" do not appear in any emails, so email searches should target "Pemberton" or general appraisal terms to surface Grace's email to Pemberton about rural turn times. This should surface: (1) Slack messages from Elena Marchetti flagging a pileup of Richard Pemberton appraisals with 2+ rural files sitting 5-7 days late, (2) Darnell Price replying that LN-2026-00197 in Rowan county is "dead in the water" until the appraisal lands and Pemberton told him "by Friday" last week but nothing came in, (3) CRM engagement notes from the vendor status review call Elena and Keisha confirming at least 4 active files affected by Pemberton delays, the borrower on LN-2026-00197 already asked if they should switch lenders, and agreement that they need a backup appraiser before cutting Richard, (4) CRM follow-up note confirming no viable backup appraiser exists (nearest alt is 2 counties over and not on approved panel), Grace reaching out to Triad Appraisal Group but no answer for at least a week, and (5) Grace's email to Pemberton (rpemberton@piedmontval.com) demanding updated status on open Keystone orders and realistic delivery timing discoverable via search_emails with query "Pemberton" or "appraisal" or "turn times".
OE 7: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find file-level history and notes across the six loans. For Destiny Pham and Taylor Morgan, filter by their CRM contact_ids (e.g., crm_crmcontact_01a70614f43b or crm_crmcontact_5611341df3bc for Pham, crm_crmcontact_437805f6883a for Morgan). For the Brian Harris / Pemberton appraisal situation, note that Brian Harris does not have a CRM contact record the relevant CRM engagements are filed under Richard Pemberton's contact ID (crm_contact_30c8c44c281b) and would be discovered by searching for Pemberton's contact or by browsing engagements that reference LN-2026-00197 in their body text. This should surface: (1) Destiny Pham-note about borrower statement that Carlos told her Frank DeLuca was the only appraiser available, the Camille/Carlos/Sofia call about lock expiration and extension pricing (0.125-0.25 points), and appraisal delay update note; (2) Taylor Morgan gift docs condition logged by UW (donor stmt for withdrawal + Taylor stmt showing deposit), and borrower pushback on donor statement; (3) Pemberton / LN-2026-00197-vendor status review call notes (crm_engagement_c5159cec305d: Elena and Keisha confirming at least 4 active files affected, borrower on LN-2026-00197 asked about switching lenders) and coverage bottleneck confirmation (crm_engagement_91f65d0a5f7c: no viable backup appraiser, Grace reaching out to Triad Appraisal Group).
OE 8: Search Slack channels using channels_list or similar to identify the processing channel (#loan-processing, channel ID C002) for posting the reassignment heads-up, and also identify #closings (C003) for any closing-related updates needed.
OE 9: Log the reassignment of the active (non-closed) loans from Marcus Webb's pipeline using mortgage_los_add_activity on each loan (LN-2026-00616, LN-2026-00539, LN-2026-00547, LN-2026-00631, and LN-2026-00197) with action: "reassignment" and detail noting the transfer from Marcus Webb to Aiden Park, and also use mortgage_los_update_loan to update the notes field on each loan recording the new LO assignment. LN-2026-00596 (Walker) is already closed and does not need reassignment. Note: the mortgage_los_update_loan tool does not support directly changing the assigned_lo field; the reassignment is recorded via activity log entries and loan notes.
OE 10: Send emails or Slack DMs to the processors on the active files to notify them of the reassignment and what needs to move first. Specifically: (1) message to Sofia Reyes (sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com) re LN-2026-00539 (Hassan)-confirm status of conditional approval conditions and closing date, (2) message to Elena Marchetti (elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com) re LN-2026-00616 (Pham) the LOS still shows Rachel Kim (inactive) as assigned processor, but Grace directed reassignment to Elena via Slack on 3/22, so Elena is the current working processor; confirm current suspension status, lender reinstatement progress, and lock recovery plan. Also re LN-2026-00547 (Grant) which also shows Rachel Kim as assigned processor and may need formal processor reassignment, (3) message to Darnell Price (darnell.price@keystonemortgage.com) re LN-2026-00631 (Morgan)-confirm status of the outstanding donor bank statement and next steps on the gift doc condition, and re LN-2026-00197 (Harris)-confirm appraisal status with Pemberton and whether there's an updated ETA.
OE 11: Post a heads-up message in the #loan-processing Slack channel (channel C002) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002") notifying the team that Aiden is picking up Marcus Webb's six files (LN-2026-00616, LN-2026-00539, LN-2026-00547, LN-2026-00631, LN-2026-00197, LN-2026-00596), summarizing file statuses and flagging the top-priority items: Pham file is suspended with realtor escalation, Harris file is blocked on appraisal, and Morgan file has outstanding gift doc condition.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search the LOS for the six loan files assigned to Marcus Webb using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan numbers: LN-2026-00616, LN-2026-00539, LN-2026-00547, LN-2026-00631, LN-2026-00197, LN-2026-00596) or mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to matching Marcus Webb's staff ID los_staff_a583f044387a) to retrieve loan status, borrower info, processor assignments, rate locks, closing dates, and current pipeline state. This should surface: Destiny Pham (LN-2026-00616) in processing with rate lock expired 3/31 and assigned processor Rachel Kim (inactive), Emily Hassan (LN-2026-00539) in conditional approval with 3/30 closing and processor Sofia Reyes, Lakshmi Grant (LN-2026-00547) in underwriting with 3/28 closing and assigned processor Rachel Kim (inactive), Taylor Morgan (LN-2026-00631) in processing with no closing date and processor Darnell Price, Brian Harris (LN-2026-00197) in underwriting with no closing date and rate lock expired 3/18 and processor Darnell Price, and James Walker (LN-2026-00596) as closed.
OE 2: Pull document checklists on all six loans using mortgage_los_get_documents (filtered by the respective loan IDs) and check for any open conditions using mortgage_los_get_conditions. This should reveal: LN-2026-00616 (Pham) has multiple docs still in "required" status (urla, paystub_2, bank_statement_2, tax_return_prior, title_commitment) while the hoi_binder shows as approved on the checklist; LN-2026-00631 (Taylor Morgan) has a standard document checklist with no gift-specific document types the gift documentation issue (donor bank statement, Taylor's deposit statement) is not tracked in the LOS checklist and would need to be discovered via email, Slack, and CRM; LN-2026-00197 (Brian Harris) shows the appraisal checklist item as received the ongoing appraisal delay with Richard Pemberton is not reflected in the LOS checklist status and would need to be discovered via Slack and CRM. The mortgage_los_get_conditions calls return no condition rows for these loans; the lender PTC conditions and underwriting requirements are tracked in email and Slack communications rather than in the LOS conditions table.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Destiny Pham" or "LN-2026-00616" or "closing failure" or "Allison Mercer" or similar) to find the full history on the Pham file: (1) the lender PTC conditions email from Homestead Wholesale to Sofia Reyes requiring updated HOI binder by 3/17 or face suspension, (2) Allison Mercer's angry email to Carlos Rivera (CC: Grace) threatening counsel and broker escalation over the missed closing and lock expiration, (3) Grace Yamamoto's reply to Allison taking over escalation as single point of contact, (4) the appraisal report from Donna Whitfield showing value supports at $291,000 but flagging foundation cracking and crawl space water damage, and (5) Grace's email scheduling a pipeline review meeting with Sofia, Elena, and Carlos to walk workload and the missed condition timeline.
OE 4: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history (query: "LN-2026-00616" or "Destiny Pham" or "suspended" or similar, channel: C002 #loan-processing or C003 #closings) to find the processing channel history on the Pham file: Sofia flagging the HOI binder condition on 3/9, Sofia admitting on 3/14 she hasn't touched it and saying the lender due date is that day (note: the PTC conditions email from the lender states 3/17 as the deadline Sofia may be referencing an internal or informal earlier target), Camille noting the file flipped to suspended on 3/19 and referencing the lock as expired (note: Slack messages reference a 3/23 lock expiration while the LOS shows rate_lock_expiration as 3/31 this is a cross-service data discrepancy the agent would encounter), Carlos noting Destiny emailed asking if they're closing that day, Sofia admitting on 3/22 she missed it entirely (file suspended, lock dead, closing passed), Carlos flagging Allison's threatening email, Grace directing Carlos to keep borrower comms tight and not promise a close date or lock fix, and Grace moving the file to Elena Marchetti as new processor.
OE 5: Search emails and Slack using search_emails (query: "Taylor Morgan" or "LN-2026-00631" or "gift" or "donor statement" or similar) and conversations_search_messages (query: "Taylor" or "gift" or "donor" or "LN-2026-00631") to find the doc situation on the Morgan file: (1) Taylor's email to Elena confirming the signed gift letter for the $20K parent gift is already in the file, (2) Amy Chen's email to Taylor explaining underwriting still needs the donor bank statement showing the $20K withdrawal and Taylor's statement showing the deposit, (3) Amy's softer follow-up email explaining parents can redact unrelated transactions while still providing the required pages, (4) Slack messages from Amy (keystone_4d01ebef3a15) noting Taylor pushed back parents won't send statement citing privacy, and Elena (keystone_c6b42763fb1f) noting the donor statement is still not in and marked pending in the checklist, and (5) CRM engagement notes logging the gift docs condition and borrower pushback.
OE 6: Search Slack and emails using conversations_search_messages (query: "Pemberton" or "LN-2026-00197" or "appraisal" or "rural" or similar, channel: C002 #loan-processing) and search_emails (query: "Pemberton" or "appraisal" or "rural" or similar) to find the appraisal delay history on the Harris file. Note: the Harris/LN-2026-00197 appraisal delay story is primarily in Slack and CRM "Brian Harris" and "LN-2026-00197" do not appear in any emails, so email searches should target "Pemberton" or general appraisal terms to surface Grace's email to Pemberton about rural turn times. This should surface: (1) Slack messages from Elena Marchetti flagging a pileup of Richard Pemberton appraisals with 2+ rural files sitting 5-7 days late, (2) Darnell Price replying that LN-2026-00197 in Rowan county is "dead in the water" until the appraisal lands and Pemberton told him "by Friday" last week but nothing came in, (3) CRM engagement notes from the vendor status review call Elena and Keisha confirming at least 4 active files affected by Pemberton delays, the borrower on LN-2026-00197 already asked if they should switch lenders, and agreement that they need a backup appraiser before cutting Richard, (4) CRM follow-up note confirming no viable backup appraiser exists (nearest alt is 2 counties over and not on approved panel), Grace reaching out to Triad Appraisal Group but no answer for at least a week, and (5) Grace's email to Pemberton (rpemberton@piedmontval.com) demanding updated status on open Keystone orders and realistic delivery timing discoverable via search_emails with query "Pemberton" or "appraisal" or "turn times".

For Destiny Pham and Taylor Morgan, filter by their CRM contact_ids (e.g., crm_crmcontact_01a70614f43b or crm_crmcontact_5611341df3bc for Pham, crm_crmcontact_437805f6883a for Morgan). OE 8: Search Slack channels using channels_list or similar to identify the processing channel (#loan-processing, channel ID C002) for posting the reassignment heads-up, and also identify #closings (C003) for any closing-related updates needed.
OE 9: Log the reassignment of the active (non-closed) loans from Marcus Webb's pipeline using mortgage_los_add_activity on each loan (LN-2026-00616, LN-2026-00539, LN-2026-00547, LN-2026-00631, and LN-2026-00197) with action: "reassignment" and detail noting the transfer from Marcus Webb to Aiden Park, and also use mortgage_los_update_loan to update the notes field on each loan recording the new LO assignment. OE 10: Send emails or Slack DMs to the processors on the active files to notify them of the reassignment and what needs to move first. Specifically: (1) message to Sofia Reyes (sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com) re LN-2026-00539 (Hassan)-confirm status of conditional approval conditions and closing date, (2) message to Elena Marchetti (elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com) re LN-2026-00616 (Pham) the LOS still shows Rachel Kim (inactive) as assigned processor, but Grace directed reassignment to Elena via Slack on 3/22, so Elena is the current working processor; confirm current suspension status, lender reinstatement progress, and lock recovery plan. Also re LN-2026-00547 (Grant) which also shows Rachel Kim as assigned processor and may need formal processor reassignment, (3) message to Darnell Price (darnell.price@keystonemortgage.com) re LN-2026-00631 (Morgan)-confirm status of the outstanding donor bank statement and next steps on the gift doc condition, and re LN-2026-00197 (Harris)-confirm appraisal status with Pemberton and whether there's an updated ETA.
OE 11: Post a heads-up message in the #loan-processing Slack channel (channel C002) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002") notifying the team that Aiden is picking up Marcus Webb's six files (LN-2026-00616, LN-2026-00539, LN-2026-00547, LN-2026-00631, LN-2026-00197, LN-2026-00596), summarizing file statuses and flagging the top-priority items: Pham file is suspended with realtor escalation, Harris file is blocked on appraisal, and Morgan file has outstanding gift doc condition.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 A loan activity was added (via mortgage_los_add_activity) to loan LN-2026-00616 (Destiny Pham) with action "reassignment" or a note referencing reassignment from Marcus Webb to Aiden Park.
outcome

✓Run #1The agent successfully called mortgage_los_add_activity on loan los_loan_66db84a42390 (LN-2026-00616) with action 'note' and detail starting with 'PIPELINE REASSIGNMENT: File reassigned from Marcus Webb to Aiden Park'. The tool returned success with a valid activity ID.

17 The final response identifies that Grace Yamamoto ([grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com](mailto:grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com)) took over escalation handling on the Pham file and/or reassigned the file from Rachel Kim to Elena Marchetti as the new processor.
outcome

✓Run #3The final response states 'Grace intervened: moved processing to Elena [Marchetti], kept Carlos borrower-facing' and also notes the processor in LOS is 'Rachel Kim (inactive/terminated), but Grace already moved it to Elena Marchetti.' Both facts are present.
✓Run #4The final response states 'Grace stepped in, replied to Allison, moved the file to Elena as processor, and told Carlos to draft an apology but not promise any dates.' It also shows processor as 'Rachel Kim (inactive) → Elena Marchetti (reassigned by Grace)'.
